D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Prism Refraction — Geometric Deviation

For a prism of apex angle A and index n, deviation δ = θᵢ + arcsin[n sin(A − arcsin(sin θᵢ/n))] − A; minimum deviation occurs at symmetric passage

Minimum deviation: sin((δmin + A)/2) = n · sin(A/2). Used to measure n: rotate prism until image stops moving, measure δmin. At symmetric passage the path through the prism is parallel to the base — useful in spectrometers and binoculars.

Slide apex angle and entry angle.
